1.11a: Contact Mortgage team via Email: conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
1.11b: Contact Mortgage team via Email: conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
1.7: Lending is not permitted when the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
Contact Mortgage team at 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk for details.
1.14: Firms may act if the seller is the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member provided the firm is a partnership.
A separate, unrelated partner must act for the lender.
No conflict of interest must exist.
The firm must adhere to professional guidelines.
1.15: Firms may act if the borrower is the person dealing with the transaction or an immediate family member provided the firm is a partnership.
A separate, unrelated partner must act for the lender.
No conflict of interest must exist.
The firm must adhere to professional guidelines.
3.1.3: The name and address of the seller's legal representative are not required.
3.1.4: Notification of an unrepresented seller is not required.
Conveyancers must ensure compliance with Section 22 of the Solicitors Act 1974.
Undertakings from unqualified conveyancers, such as mortgage discharges, must not be accepted.
3.1.5: Electronic verification of identity and address is preferred.
If electronic verification fails, one document from List 1 (photo ID) and one from List 2 (address) are required.
List 1: Valid passport, valid full UK Driving License, or EU member state ID card.

List 2: Utility bill (< 3 months old), Driving License (if not used for ID), Council tax bill (current year), Government department letter (< 3 months old), non-Perenna mortgage statement (most recent), or bank/credit card statement (< 3 months old).
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.2.4: Notification of an unrepresented seller is not required.
Compliance with Section 22 of the Solicitors Act 1974 and professional guidance is required.
Undertakings from unqualified conveyancers must be rejected.
4.5a: Contact Mortgage team for re-inspections: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
4.5b: Certificates of Title must be submitted electronically via the Lender Exchange Portal.
Submit the Certificate of Title with a minimum of 5 full working days' notice before completion.
Confirm with the customer that all pre-completion queries are resolved before submission.
Exception process for less than 5 days' notice requires contact via 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk with rationale.
4.1: Physical valuation reports are for lending purposes and are not shared as standard.
Online or electronic valuation reports are not available for sharing.
Contact the Mortgage team for report requests by exception.
4.3: Report incorrect valuer assumptions to the Mortgage team: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.1.1: Report cases where the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.2.1: Report cases where the seller is not the owner or registered proprietor to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are only required if specified in the mortgage offer, requested by the valuer, or indicate an adverse effect on security.
Reports affecting security must be forwarded with a recommendation.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the agent is reputable, subscribes to the search code (PCCB monitored), is COPSO registered, and holds adequate professional indemnity insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for re-mortgage applications at the conveyancer's risk.
Insurance must protect the lender and allow for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.5.3a: Report evidence of breach or unsatisfied conditions to the Mortgage team: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.5.3b: Original or copies of planning permission are not required unless the property was converted within the last 10 years.
A copy must be retained in the mortgage file in all cases.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required unless the property was converted within the last 10 years.
A copy must be retained in the mortgage file in all cases.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use are only required if the property was converted from commercial to residential use.
5.5.4: Report restrictions affecting value or marketability to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if the element does not exceed 20% of the property.
Adequate rights of support and mutually enforceable repairing covenants must exist.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not acceptable.
Exceptions include: share of freehold with a leasehold interest, blocks where other flats are leasehold, or Tyneside flat arrangements.
Other cases must be referred to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.7.1c: No reporting is required if 5.7 conditions are met.
Clarification requests should go to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.8.1: Security comprising a building converted into max four flats where the borrower owns the freehold and occupies a flat is not accepted.
5.8.5: Arrangements where the borrower and another leaseholder own each other's freehold reversions are accepted.
A charge is required over both the leasehold and the freehold reversion titles owned by the borrower.
5.10.1: Report use restrictions to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.13.1: Gifted deposits from non-family members must be reported to the Mortgage team.
A standard form gifted deposit declaration must be signed confirming the gift is non-refundable and unconditional with no interest claimed.
Conveyancers must verify the donor's source of funds, source of wealth, and identity documents.
Existing charges securing gifted deposits must be redeemed on completion.
Back-to-back transactions (purchase/remortgage within 6 months) are only accepted by exception (e.g., probate, developer sales).
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.14.15b: Contact the Mortgage team regarding the acceptability of indemnity insurance for absent landlords.
5.14.17a: Documentation does not need to be sent unless specifically requested before completion.
5.14.17b: Building safety documentation must comply with the Building Safety Act 2022.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term of 85 years required at mortgage commencement.
5.14.9: Contact the Mortgage team for lease matters: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.14.10: Contact the Mortgage team for service charge matters: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms is accepted if it resolves the issue and allows for an unqualified Certificate of Title.
Policy copies must be retained on file.
5.14.12: Ground rent must not exceed 0.5% of the property value.
Ground rent escalation intervals must be 10 years or greater.
Ground rent escalation must be linked to the Retail Price Index (RPI).
Estate Rent Charges exceeding £500 per annum must be referred for review.
Lease extensions/freehold purchases are accepted if completed simultaneously and meeting policy.
5.14.13: Evidence of service of notice is not required to be sent but must be kept on file.
5.15.2a: Report management company issues to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms should be retained on file, not sent.
5.15.2d: Memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Contact Mortgage team if unable to certify a search entry does not relate: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.17.5a: Power of Attorney documents must be kept on file, not sent.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation must be kept on file, not sent.
5.19.1: Contact Mortgage team regarding affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.20.1: Report solar panel roof space leases not meeting UK Finance minimums to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
5.20.3: Solar panels are accepted if bought outright or via additional borrowing without a long-term lease of the roof/air space.
Leases with the panel provider taking grid tariff income are not accepted.
A lease may be considered if a break clause of 6 months or less exists and marketability is not affected.
5.20.4: Report existing Green Deal Plans to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.1.3: Notify Mortgage team if the borrower is not proceeding: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.4.4: Report incentives unless: New build cash incentive is <= 5% of price/valuation AND LTV is <= 90% (excluding fees).
Non-cash incentives (white goods, carpets, kitchen upgrades) for new builds do not need reporting.
Cash incentives include stamp duty contributions, fee payments, and gifted deposits.
6.4.5: Contact Mortgage team if the conveyancer lacks control over purchase money: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.5.1: Report absence of vacant possession to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.6.1: Report property letting to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk; Buy-to-let is currently not offered.
6.6.2: Contact Mortgage team for missing letting details: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not permitted.
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plan, LABC, Checkmate Castle 10, Build Assure, Build Zone, Zurich Municipal, UK Finance PCC with PI proof, Advantage, Aedis, Global, Protek, ICW, Ark Residential, The Q Policy, One Guarantee.
6.7.2: Warranty documentation must be kept on file, not sent.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be kept on file, not sent.
6.7.4: Applications cannot proceed if a new build lacks a required warranty scheme.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates must be kept on file, not sent.
6.8.1: Roads and sewers serving the property must be adopted or covered by a suitable agreement/bond.
6.9.1: Absence of necessary easements must be reported to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
Adjoining land owned by the borrower used for services/access must also be charged.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments of the advance are not offered.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights or resale restrictions to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
7.3: Occupiers Consent Form required for all occupants aged 17 or over.
Form is available on the Lender Exchange document section.
7.4: Report information accuracy doubts to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
8.1: Contact Mortgage team regarding advice to third parties: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be kept on file, not sent.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the higher of the purchase price or valuation.
10.2a: Advance is paid electronically via FPS, typically 1 working day before the completion date.
Funds reach accounts immediately but can take up to 24 hours.
Same-day funding is only by exception if notice is less than 5 days.
10.2b: 5 full working days' notice required for Certificate of Title.
Exception process for shorter notice: 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
10.3: Standard deductions include the FPS transfer fee and fees specified in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: Funds must be returned if completion is delayed by more than 2 working days from the date on the Certificate of Title.
Return funds via FPS or CHAPS to: Perenna, Sort Code 20-00-00, Account 93737225, quoting application number.
New 5 working days' notice required if funds are returned.
10.9: Report completion delays to 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk
10.10: Maximum hold period for funds is 48 hours post-completion date; thereafter they must be returned.
10.11: Interest is charged from the date funds are initially released.
12.3.1: Contact 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk for retention/instalment matters.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds and title documents must be retained by the solicitor and provided on request.
14.2.1: Retain hard copies of title deeds/documents; provide to Perenna only upon request.
14.2.2: No documents are required post-completion unless requested.
16.1.1: Contact Mortgage Servicing for title documents: 0333 038 2430 or admin@servicing.perenna.com
Solicitor undertaking required to hold deeds and return within 5 working days of request.
16.3.7a: Contact 0330 341 2013 or conveyancing@perenna.co.uk for execution of transfer equity.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: SIGNED AS A DEED in the presence of a witness (including name, address, and signature).
16.3.1: Lender consent is required before any transfer takes place.
16.3.2: Contact Mortgage Servicing for debt amounts: 0333 038 2430 or admin@servicing.perenna.com
16.3.4: Transfer of equity only needs to be sent if the lender must execute it.
Prior consent from Perenna is mandatory.
16.4.1: Contact Mortgage Servicing for consent to letting: 0333 038 2430 or admin@servicing.perenna.com
16.4.2: Tenancy copies not required; Buy-to-Let is not currently offered and all requests must be referred to Servicing.
16.5.3a: Send Deed of Variation to admin@servicing.perenna.com
16.5.3b: Send Deed of Rectification to admin@servicing.perenna.com
16.5.3c: Send Deed of Easement to admin@servicing.perenna.com
16.5.2: Contact Mortgage Servicing to confirm if agreements affect security: 0333 038 2430 or admin@servicing.perenna.com
17.1.1: Contact Mortgage Servicing for redemption statements: 0333 038 2430 or admin@servicing.perenna.com
17.2.1a: Discharge and remittance details are provided in the redemption statement.
17.2.1b: Discharges are processed directly with Land Registry via eDS1.
DS1 forms are used only in exceptional circumstances.
